home.hero_headline: Securing critical networks, beyond software limits.
home.hero_sub: Pesaba develops hardware products for controlled data transfer, network encryption, segmentation, and infrastructure monitoring. Capabilities and build scope are documented per model.
home.products_sub: Six product families for controlling and measuring critical networks.
home.cta_headline: Lay a secure network foundation with Pesaba
home.explore_all_products_solutions: Explore our full range of products & solutions

products.title: Products
products.index_sub: Products across one-way data transfer, network encryption, monitoring, switching, telecom transmission, and water biomonitoring.
products.direct_datasheet_note: This opens the exact datasheet for this product.
products.call_for_datasheet: For the latest datasheet, call sales engineering directly.

glossary.subtitle: Technical terms, defined.
glossary.sub: Technical dictionary for network security, encryption, OT/ICS, telecom, and cellular monitoring.

trust.title: Trust Center
trust.headline: Technical transparency for security decision-makers
trust.compliance_sub: Publication status of supporting evidence and the distinction between technical alignment, product certification, and organisational certification.
trust.compliance_note: A reference to a standard is not certification; the model, version, scope, and evidence must be identified.
trust.sub: Product architecture, evidence status, build chain, and service terms with a clear boundary between public information and contractual commitments.
trust.certifications_desc: No product certification is presented as verified without an evidence number, authority, scope, model, version, and validity status.
trust.fpga_desc: Avoiding a general-purpose operating system in some architectures can reduce software attack surface; the applicable model specification must confirm the design.
trust.supply_chain_desc: The scope of design, assembly, testing, and component origin can be confirmed for a specific product and order.
trust.aes_desc: AES-256 or hardware implementation claims apply only to models whose approved specifications identify those capabilities.
trust.sla_desc: Warranty period, response targets, and service level are defined in the applicable proposal or project contract.
trust.parts_desc: Production, repair, and parts availability depend on model, order timing, and supply conditions and must be confirmed before purchase.

contact.title: Contact
contact.headline: Talk to our team
contact.sub: For product quotes, technical demos, or guidance on selecting the right solution for your network.
contact.phone_first_title: Fastest path: call our sales team
contact.phone_first_sub: Pricing, product fit, and availability are usually confirmed faster by phone.
contact.success_sent: Your request was sent. We'll be in touch shortly.

careers.send_cv_sub: If you have expertise in any of the following areas and a passion for hardware security, we'd like to hear from you:

why_cards.title: Iranian Product Development
why_cards.body: Pesaba develops network-security products in Iran. Manufacturing scope, component origin, and supply-chain documentation are confirmed for each product and procurement project.

why_cards.title: Purpose-built Architectures
why_cards.body: Architecture, operating-system presence, and data-path design vary by product. Use the current product datasheet and project documentation for technical decisions.

why_cards.title: Evidence-based Compliance
why_cards.body: Certification and compliance status are only confirmed against current, product-specific evidence. Relevant documents can be requested during technical or procurement review.

why_cards.title: Engineering-led Review
why_cards.body: Product selection, deployment requirements, and available technical documentation are reviewed with the relevant engineering or sales team.

industries.title: Industries
industries.sub: Deployment patterns for power, telecom, water, government, defense, finance, and manufacturing networks.
industries.index_sub: Pesaba network security solutions for every sector — data diodes, encryption, and monitoring.

solutions.headline: Mission-fit solutions for critical infrastructure
solutions.sub: Browse deployment paths by industry and operational scenario, from SCADA isolation to cellular quality monitoring.
solutions.industry_desc_power_grid: Substation segmentation, OT/IT isolation, and one-way monitoring flows.
solutions.industry_desc_telecom: KPI measurement, drive test, and secure telemetry for operator networks.
solutions.industry_desc_government: Hardware encryption and domain separation for high-assurance networks.
solutions.industry_desc_water: Water quality monitoring and real-time alerting for essential utilities.
solutions.usecase_desc_one_way: One-way transfer patterns using the documented hardware boundary of the selected model.
solutions.usecase_desc_ot_it: Hardware-enforced segmentation between operational and enterprise networks.
solutions.usecase_desc_aes: Hardware AES encryption options for supported backbone and inter-site links.
solutions.usecase_desc_cellular: Coverage and KPI analysis from field probes to centralized reporting.

technology.headline: Product-specific hardware controls for sensitive networks
technology.sub: Pesaba products use different combinations of FPGA logic, dedicated hardware, management interfaces, and software services. Review the exact model boundary before deployment.
technology.compare_desc: Review architecture choices, link rates, and form factors side by side.
technology.trust_desc: Inspect evidence status, build-chain scope, and the terms that require project-specific confirmation.
technology.pillar_fpga_desc: Applicable model specifications identify which functions are implemented in dedicated FPGA logic.
technology.pillar_osless_desc: Some models avoid a general-purpose operating system on defined paths; all exposed interfaces still require assessment.
technology.pillar_oneway_desc: For one-way products, the selected model's architecture and acceptance tests define the return-path constraint.
technology.pillar_local_desc: Design, assembly, testing, and component-origin scope are confirmed for the selected product and order.
technology.architecture_summary: Pesaba technology must be legible to professional buyers: traceable data paths, small attack surface, and predictable hardware behavior.
technology.design_principles_title: The decisions that show up at product level
technology.data_architecture_desc: In key product lines, the security boundary is enforced in hardware. The data path is defined in FPGA logic, not in an operating system or software configuration.
technology.network_filtering_title: Traffic control at the hardware layer
technology.network_filtering_desc: Packet filtering runs on the FPGA without a general-purpose CPU in the path. This ensures filtering decisions stay out of the software stack that can be exploited.
technology.proof_1_desc: Professional buyers need to see how packets move, where controls live, and what can or cannot execute on the platform.
technology.proof_2_desc: Key product lines avoid general-purpose operating systems and shrink the surrounding software perimeter.
technology.proof_3_desc: From SCADA isolation to inter-site encryption, the form factor and logic are chosen around the actual job to be done.

resources.headline: Technical resources, research, and support material
resources.sub: Browse articles, glossary terms, datasheets, and firmware resources from one place.
resources.glossary_desc: Reference terms for networking, telecom, and security.
resources.datasheets_desc: Product documents and paths to technical specifications.
resources.datasheets_sub: Current product specifications are shown on each product page. Contact technical support to request an available formal document and confirm its model and version scope.
resources.firmware_desc: Official route to check versions, request a package, and obtain device-specific installation guidance.
resources.signed_pdf_desc: For procurement, the available document, covered model and version, and any supporting evidence are confirmed after the request is reviewed.
resources.firmware_page_sub: Update availability, package type, verification method, and installation process depend on the product model and hardware revision. Obtain packages and instructions only through the official support route.
resources.firmware_requirements_title: What we need to release firmware
resources.firmware_req_org: Organisation name and technical contact details
resources.firmware_req_reason: Reason for the update (bug fix, new feature, security compliance)
resources.firmware_policy_signed: Support identifies the official authenticity or integrity check for each available package; verification methods are not identical across models and generations.
resources.firmware_policy_critical: The route and coverage of security communications depend on the product, registered contact details, and the applicable support or contract terms.
resources.firmware_policy_jtag: Installation may involve a field update, an engineer visit, or return of the device. Do not flash or use JTAG without written instructions for the exact model.
resources.firmware_sensitive_notice: Do not submit passwords, cryptographic keys, configuration files, full network diagrams, operational data, or classified information through the public form. Coordinate an appropriate exchange channel first if needed.

assistant.title: Pesaba product selector
assistant.intro: Choose a category to view active products and the specifications published on their pages. This tool does not replace a project-specific technical assessment.
assistant.fallback: Product fit, compatibility, availability, and final specifications must be confirmed for your project by sales engineering.

meta.default_description: Pesaba data diodes, network encryptors, switching products, telecom equipment, and monitoring tools, with model-specific specifications and procurement evidence.
